purpose: Project-level instructions auto-loaded by Claude Code when working inside this repository. Acts as the canonical, condensed orientation document for any Claude agent operating on Synapse itself — it explains that Synapse is a distributed agent swarm control system, defines the {tracker_root} vs {project_root} path convention, summarizes how master/worker orchestration writes JSON files that Watcher/SSE/IPC pipelines stream into the React dashboard, and lists every command the user can invoke. Functionally, this is the agent-facing spec for the entire project — it overrides default behavior and is treated as authoritative by Claude Code's harness.
gotchas: Despite the project being self-targeted (.synapse/project.json points at /Users/dean/Desktop/Synapse), this file describes the architecture as if running against an external target — when Synapse is its own target, {tracker_root} and {project_root} resolve to the same path.

The 'Quick Start' block tells users to run `!project set /path/...` and then `npm start`, but `npm start` runs `vite build && electron .` — there is no separate server process; the SSE server runs INSIDE Electron's main process (per Synapse.md and package.json main field).

CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md overlap heavily but are not synonyms — Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md as project instructions automatically; AGENTS.md is a longer master-protocol spec that is read on demand. Edits to one without the other will drift.

The directory listing claims `tasks/{date}/` is legacy/deprecated and that planning lives in `dashboards/{id}/plan.json`, but other docs (AGENTS.md, README.md) still reference `tasks/{date}/parallel_*.json` as live master writes. Verify before relying on either path.

Mandatory full-tracking thresholds (3+ agents OR multi-wave) are stated as non-negotiable — this is a behavioral constraint enforced by the agent itself, not by tooling, so a careless `!p` invocation can silently violate it.
patterns: Doc-as-config: this file is loaded by the harness (NOT by application code) — the 'IMPORTANT: These instructions OVERRIDE any default behavior' lead is the trigger that elevates it to system-prompt status.
Path convention pattern: every operational doc in the repo uses {tracker_root} / {project_root} placeholders rather than concrete paths to keep agent instructions portable across machines.
Layered protocol pattern: CLAUDE.md (short orientation) → AGENTS.md (full protocol) → agent/master/*.md + agent/worker/*.md (deep references) → .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md (loaded on demand).
Mode taxonomy: Serial / Parallel-tracked (`!p_track`) / Lightweight-parallel (`!p`) / Multi-stream (`!master_plan_track`) — every command in the repo maps onto one of these execution modes.
conventions: ASCII tree diagrams use `|--`, `+--`, `+-->` (no Unicode box-drawing) so the file renders identically in plain terminals, GitHub, and Claude Code.
Command names are always written with a leading `!` (e.g., `!p_track`).
Tables list one command per row with a single 'Description' column — kept concise so the file fits in the context window.
Quick-Start code fences use ```bash and assume a macOS shell.
Directory annotations after `<--` describe the file's role in 1 short sentence.
